title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs · Claim Audit: Performance Budget
summary: The team optimizes synthetic speed while real users face slow devices, large payloads, queue growth, resource contention, and unpredictable bills. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 performance budget increment in capacity and cost evidence model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.
Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.

accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Performance Budget · Theory Performance Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Performance Budget contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Performance Budget · Theory Performance Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, cpu, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions. For Predict Performance Budget · Theory Performance Budget, keep Performance Budget connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Performance Budget while producing capacity and cost evidence model.

Competency probe: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions. decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context

failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence

steps.title: Defend the Performance Budget model · Theory Performance Budget
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Performance Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Defend the Performance Budget model · Theory Performance Budget, keep Performance Budget connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
